system.description:
Orks are big, blunt, and built like the world owes them an apology. They mature fast, hit harder, and have a kind of cellular durability that makes the gene boutiques weep with envy — an ork can walk away from injuries that would kill three humans and a vending machine. That toughness is paired with a short metabolic clock; ork lives burn bright and end early, and ork culture has organized itself around that fact with an honesty most of the other metatypes can't match.

The sprawl is full of orks because the sprawl is where the work is, and the work is usually the kind that needs someone willing to take a hit. They run security, demolition, heavy rigging, frontline shadow ops — anywhere a strong back and a stronger jaw matter more than a polished resume. Outsiders mistake their bluntness for stupidity, which is convenient, because an ork with a good poker face and a grudge is exactly the kind of problem the corps never see coming.

wizard.tagline: Built for a world that hits back
